title: Production Operations, Incidents, Recovery, and Release Defense · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: An engineering, security, accessibility, privacy, compliance, and operations board. The board needs an algorithm and key inventory, Go 1.26 and x/crypto compatibility evidence, FIPS-status accuracy, vectors and fuzzing, interop, native side-channel scope, capacity and dependency faults, canary migration, rollback, compromise response, restore drills, and residual-risk ownership. Planned artifact: Deliver Cryptographic Platform Production Defense with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflect…
objectives: Maintain machine-readable use, purpose, owner, suite, key location, dependency, data lifetime, protocol, version, and migration state for cryptographic assets.
Distinguish approved algorithms, Go cryptographic module version, GOFIPS140 selection, enforcement, validation status, operating environment, and application boundary.
Cross-test supported Go versions, architectures, algorithms, parameter sets, external implementations, old data, malformed data, and rollout states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Crypto Go Algorithm Inventory
steps.instruction:
project case — A project cryptographic engineering team handles case 8836 during a credential migration window. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate release on deterministic, vector, fuzz, interoperability, native side-channel, load, rollback, and recovery evidence; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then change one authenticated byte and prove clean rejection without partial effect. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Crypto Go Algorithm Inventory.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for maintain machine-readable use, purpose, owner, suite, key location, dependency, data lifetime, protocol, version, and migration state for cryptographic assets. For Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Crypto Go Algorithm Inventory, keep Crypto Go Algorithm Inventory connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Crypto Go Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crypto-go-production-defense-1at9cd" that uses case 5007 to gate release on deterministic, vector, fuzz, interoperability, native side-channel, load, rollback, and recovery evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import cryptographic packages, generate or handle production keys, open sockets, contact TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, compliance services, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, standard crypto APIs, authoritative vectors, fuzz, race, native side-channel measurement, interoperability, controlled network and key-custody systems, load, fault, recovery, compliance, and production gates.
